options.D: make reproductive concerns the sole responsibility of women.

options.A: avoid pointing out the mistakes of each other.
options.B: hide their past lifestyles from each other.
options.C: maintain constant communication with each other.
options.D: report each others behaviour to his or her parents.

questions.question_text: Which of the following options must not be pursued as a purpose of marriage in the Ghanaian society?

options.A: A means for the couple to provide support for each other
options.B: Avenue for companionship among the couples
options.C: Provision of opportunity to have sexual relations with anybody
options.D: Provision of legitimate avenue for procreation

questions.question_text: The Intestate Succession Law in Ghana was promulgated to

options.A: allow the deceased's nephews to inherit property.
options.B: allow the surviving spouse to share the property.
options.C: enable the extended family to control the property.
options.D: enable survivors get their share of the property.

options.C: mountains, lowlands and rivers in our communities.
options.D: external surroundings within which all living things live.

questions.question_text: The main aim of formal education is to

options.A: help the individual secure a well-paid job.
options.B: make the individual fit well in the society.
options.C: provide the individual with higher academic excellence.
options.D: provide the individual with occupational skills.

options.A: always carry our complains to people in authority.
options.B: demonstrate our willingness to solve national problems.
options.C: show lukewarm attitude to duty and demand for our rights.
options.D: plan national development from local communities.

options.A: determine what should happen to followers.
options.B: guide others towards the attainment of a group's objectives.

options.A: exercise of absolute power over followers.
options.D: respect for the opinions of followers.

questions.question_text: Which of the following constitutional provisions prevents autocratic rule?

options.D: Rule of law and separation of powers

options.A: governments reward communities that vote for them with projects.
options.B: it promotes smooth and peaceful change of government.
options.C: only views of members of the ruling part are accommodated.
options.D: rules agreed upon are binding on political leaders.
